sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, timestamp_col TIMESTAMP, value FLOAT, text_col TEXT);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, timestamp_col, value, text_col) VALUES (1, TIMESTAMP '2023-01-01', 5.0, 'test1'), (2, TIMESTAMP '2023-01-02', NULL, 'test2'), (3, TIMESTAMP '2023-01-03', 10.5, 'test3');
CREATE TABLE second_table (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, timestamp_col TIMESTAMP, amount DOUBLE);
INSERT INTO second_table (id, timestamp_col, amount) VALUES (1, TIMESTAMP '2023-01-01', 2.0), (2, TIMESTAMP '2023-01-02', 3.5);
SELECT id, fmod(value, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM second_table)) AS mod_value FROM test_table WHERE timestamp_col - (SELECT MAX(timestamp_col) FROM second_table) > INTERVAL '1 day';
SELECT t1.id, fmod(t1.value, (SELECT fmod(MAX(amount), 1) FROM second_table t2 WHERE t1.timestamp_col > t2.timestamp_col)) AS mod_result FROM test_table t1 WHERE t1.value IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, fmod(value, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM second_table)) AS mod_result FROM test_table GROUP BY id HAVING mod_result IS NOT NULL;
SELECT t1.id, fmod(fmod(t1.value, 3), (CAST(7 AS DOUBLE) + 1)) AS complex_mod FROM test_table t1 JOIN second_table t2 ON t1.id = t2.id WHERE t1.value IS NOT NULL;
SELECT t1.id, fmod(t1.value, fmod(CAST(t1.text_col AS DOUBLE), 3)) AS final_value FROM test_table t1 WHERE t1.text_col IS NOT NULL;
SELECT t1.id, timestamp_col, fmod(fmod(value, 3), -1) AS recursive_mod FROM test_table t1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT * FROM second_table t2 WHERE t1.id = t2.id) AND fmod(value, 3) IS NOT NULL;

SELECT t1.id, (timestamp_col - (SELECT MAX(timestamp_col) FROM second_table) - INTERVAL '1 day') AS timestamp_difference FROM test_table t1 WHERE value < (SELECT AVG(amount) FROM second_table) AND value IS NOT NULL;
